title: Has Agenus (AGEN) Outpaced Other Medical Stocks This Year?
content:
Investors interested in Medical stocks should always be looking to find the best-performing companies in the group. Is Agenus (AGEN) one of those stocks right now? A quick glance at the company's year-to-date performance in comparison to the rest of the Medical sector should help us answer this question.

Agenus is one of 892 companies in the Medical group. The Medical group currently sits at #7 within the Zacks Sector Rank. The Zacks Sector Rank considers 16 different groups, measuring the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks within the sector to gauge the strength of each group.

The Zacks Rank is a proven model that highlights a variety of stocks with the right characteristics to outperform the market over the next one to three months. The system emphasizes earnings estimate revisions and favors companies with improving earnings outlooks. Agenus is currently sporting a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy).

Over the past 90 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate for AGEN's full-year earnings has moved 352.4% higher. This shows that analyst sentiment has improved and the company's earnings outlook is stronger.

Based on the latest available data, AGEN has gained about 6.1% so far this year. At the same time, Medical stocks have lost an average of 4.1%. This shows that Agenus is outperforming its peers so far this year.

One other Medical stock that has outperformed the sector so far this year is Ensign Group (ENSG). The stock is up 12.9% year-to-date.

For Ensign Group, the consensus EPS estimate for the current year has increased 5.7% over the past three months. The stock currently has a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

Looking more specifically, Agenus belongs to the Medical - Biomedical and Genetics industry, a group that includes 436 individual stocks and currently sits at #143 in the Zacks Industry Rank. Stocks in this group have gained about 5% so far this year, so AGEN is performing better this group in terms of year-to-date returns.

Ensign Group, however, belongs to the Medical - Nursing Homes industry. Currently, this 2-stock industry is ranked #4. The industry has moved +16.2% so far this year.

Investors with an interest in Medical stocks should continue to track Agenus and Ensign Group. These stocks will be looking to continue their solid performance.

title: Here are the major earnings before the open Monday
content:
Major earnings expected before the bell on Monday include:

title: Earnings week ahead: FDX, BABA, XPEV, MU, GIS, DOCU, OKLO, ACN, and more
content:
[Quarterly Report Financial Analysis and Business Performance Review with Office Tools]
Mohamad Faizal Bin Ramli/iStock via Getty Images

Wall Street has entered a quieter phase of the earnings calendar, but the macro environment is shouting. While the earnings calendar has thinned out, the upcoming week still brings key reports from a diverse set of companies across various sectors: semiconductors, cloud services, retail, logistics, and clean energy, offering investors fresh insights into global demand trends and corporate spending.

Chipmaker Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]) will be closely watched for signals on memory demand and the AI-driven data center cycle.

In Asia, results from Alibaba Group Holding Limited (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA]) and Tencent Holdings Limited (TCEHY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TCEHY]) could provide a read on China’s internet and e-commerce landscape, while EV maker XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]) will offer insight into competition within China’s fast-growing electric vehicle market.

Enterprise and professional services companies will also be in focus, including Accenture (ACN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACN]) and Docusign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]), which may shed light on corporate technology spending and digital transformation trends.

On the consumer side, retailers and brands such as lululemon athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]), Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]), and Dollar Tree (DLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR]) are expected to provide clues on discretionary spending and value-oriented shopping behavior, while packaged food giant General Mills (GIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GIS]) will offer perspective on staples demand and pricing trends.

Logistics and transportation demand will be highlighted by results from FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]), often viewed as a barometer for global trade and economic activity.

Meanwhile, clean energy and emerging technology names, including Canadian Solar (CSIQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSIQ]), Oklo (OKLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKLO]), Red Cat Holdings (RCAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RCAT]), and satellite imaging company Planet Labs PBC (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]), will round out a diverse reporting calendar.

With companies spanning technology, consumer goods, logistics, and energy, the week’s results could provide investors with a broad snapshot of economic momentum and sector-specific trends heading into the next quarter.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of March 16 to March 20:

MONDAY, MARCH 16

Dollar Tree (DLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR]) is scheduled to report FQ4 results before the market opens on Monday, with analysts expecting profits to rise more than 10% despite a roughly 34% decline in revenue.

Wall Street analysts maintain a Hold rating on the stock. Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating recently downgraded it to Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DLTR/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating] from Strong Buy ahead of earnings, citing valuation concerns.

Seeking Alpha author Caffital Research rates the stock Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880852-dollar-tree-lets-see-how-the-strategy-performs], noting that Dollar Tree is likely to post solid Q4 earnings supported by strong sales momentum and holiday-season assortments. The key focus will be the company’s FY2026 guidance, as strategic initiatives, tariff changes, and a volatile consumer environment create a somewhat uncertain but generally positive outlook. Caffital Research estimates fair value for DLTR at about $134.80 per share.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.53
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $5.47B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has exceeded EPS in 6 of the past 8 quarters and revenue in 5 of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: Agenus (AGEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AGEN]), Niu Technologies (NIU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NIU]), WW International (WW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WW]), Assertio Therapeutics (ASRT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASRT]), KE Holdings (BEKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEKE]), Science Applications International (SAIC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SAIC]), Getty Images (GETY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GETY]), and more.

TUESDAY, MARCH 17

DOCUSIGN (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU])

DocuSign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]) is set to report Q4 earnings after the market closes on Tuesday, with analysts expecting year-over-year growth in both EPS and revenue.

Recently, Needham & Company said DocuSign is testing a new pricing [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562787-docusign-ponders-new-pricing-for-esignature-pro-tier-needham] and packaging model for its eSignature Professional tier. According to analyst Scott Berg, the company is experimenting with a potential 50% price increase while raising the annual envelope limit from 100 to unlimited. Management confirmed pricing tests are underway but noted that no final decisions have been made. Needham said the impact is difficult to quantify due to limited data, though the initiative could support growth in FY2027 and FY2028. The testing does not involve Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform, its AI-powered system for workflow automation, document analysis, and agreement data integration.

Both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating maintain a Hold rating [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], reflecting caution around competition and valuation.

However, Seeking Alpha contributor Brett Ashcroft-Green maintains a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880412-docusign-earnings-preview-for-an-anti-bubble-poster-child], arguing that DocuSign's valuation has compressed significantly despite steady revenue growth and improving free cash flow. He also notes that AI disruption concerns may be overstated, as digital agreement infrastructure remains deeply embedded in enterprise software ecosystems.

Ashcroft-Green adds that expanding international adoption and growth in the IAM platform could provide new revenue streams beyond core e-signature services. With a strong balance sheet, net cash position, and high gross margins, DocuSign also has financial flexibility during a slower SaaS growth cycle.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.95
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $828.22M
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has consistently outperformed EPS and revenue expectations in the past 8 quarters.

ALSO REPORTING: lululemon athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]), HUYA (HUYA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HUYA]), Tencent Music Entertainment Group (TME [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TME]), Ocean Power Technologies (OPTT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPTT]), Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASO]), Abeona Therapeutics (ABEO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABEO]), Qifu Technology (QFIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QFIN]), IZEA (IZEA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IZEA]), HealthEquity (HQY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HQY]), ZTO Express (ZTO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZTO]), and more.

WEDNESDAY, MARCH 18

MICRON TECHNOLOGY (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU])

Micron Technology (MU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU]) is set to release FQ2 results after the closing bell on Wednesday, with analysts expecting profits to surge 452% Y/Y on 138% revenue growth, driven by strong AI-related demand for memory chips, particularly DRAM and high-bandwidth memory (HBM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBM]).

Ahead of the report, Citigroup reiterated its Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562317-micron-sees-bullish-views-at-citi-ahead-of-earnings] rating and raised its price target to $430 from $385, citing rising memory prices and robust AI demand. Susquehanna International Group also lifted its target to $525 from $345 while maintaining a positive rating.

Meanwhile, GF Securities expects the memory cycle to remain “robust, [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562649-micron-likely-to-reiterate-robust-memory-cycle-for-q2-and-beyond-gf-securities]” forecasting DRAM contract prices to jump about 100% in Q1 2026, followed by more than 30% sequential growth in Q2. Analyst Jeff Pu raised his price target to $571, projecting $23B in Q2 revenue with 77% gross margins, and sees Q3 revenue potentially reaching $29B with margins expanding further. Pu also noted Micron has likely secured early HBM4 orders.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating maintains a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MU/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], broadly aligned with bullish sell-side sentiment.

Seeking Alpha contributor Pythia Research also rates the stock a Strong Buy, pointing to strong fiscal Q1 [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4881453-microns-next-chapter-starts-after-q2] results with $13.6B revenue, 56.8% gross margin, $4.78 EPS, and a record $3.9B in free cash flow. AI-driven demand has tightened supply, with HBM capacity sold out through 2026, while high-value segments such as cloud and data-center memory continue to expand rapidly.

However, Seeking Alpha contributor Deep Value Investing downgraded the stock to Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4879327-micron-technology-stock-samsung-hbm4-memory-chip-downside-catalyst], warning that Samsung Electronics’s HBM4 production ramp could pressure Micron’s market share. After a 215% rally since last summer, the analyst argues Micron may need consistent beat-and-raise quarters to sustain momentum.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $8.61
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $19.13B
 	* Earnings Insight: Micron has beaten EPS and revenue expectations in 8 consecutive quarters.

ALSO REPORTING: Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]), General Mills (GIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GIS]), Hello Group (MOMO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MOMO]), Weibo Corporation (WB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WB]), Williams-Sonoma (WSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WSM]), Five Below (FIVE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FIVE]), Jabil (JBL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBL]), SailPoint (SAIL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SAIL]), Protalix BioTherapeutics (PLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLX]), and more.

THURSDAY, MARCH 19

ALIBABA GROUP HOLDING (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA])

Alibaba Group Holding Limited (BABA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA]) is set to report FQ3 results on Thursday before the market opens, offering a key read on China’s consumer environment and sentiment toward Chinese technology stocks. Analysts expect revenue to rise about 9% Y/Y, while profits are projected to decline roughly 45%, pointing to a mixed near-term outlook.

The company recently launched a dedicated mobile app enabling users to deploy OpenClaw within minutes, highlighting intensifying competition among Chinese tech giants in the emerging agentic AI assistant space.

Morgan Stanley recently named [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4563734-alibaba-named-as-top-pick-at-morgan-stanley-as-firm-calls-it-global-ai-winner] Alibaba its top pick among Chinese tech companies, maintaining an Overweight rating and calling it a potential “global AI winner.”

Wall Street analysts broadly rate the stock as a Strong Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating is more cautious at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BABA/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating].

Seeking Alpha contributor Oakoff Investments maintains a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880402-alibabas-q3-earnings-could-trigger-a-rebound-preview], expecting Alibaba to beat conservative Q3 estimates and provide upbeat commentary, supported by cloud growth, improving quick-commerce margins, and strong user retention. The analyst sets a $153.51 price target, implying about 15–16% upside.

However, another contributor, Geneva Investor, rates the stock a Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4880368-alibaba-macro-perspective-on-an-uninvestable-stock], citing Alibaba’s heavy reliance on China’s domestic economy and ongoing macro and geopolitical uncertainties that could weigh on long-term growth.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.59
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $42.20B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS and revenue estimates in 3 of the past 8 quarters.

ALSO REPORTING: FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]), Accenture (ACN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACN]), Canadian Solar (CSIQ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSIQ]), GrowGeneration (GRWG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRWG]), Darden Restaurants (DRI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DRI]), Signet Jewelers (SIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SIG]), UP Fintech Holding Limited (TIGR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TIGR]), Arcos Dorados Holdings Inc. (ARCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARCO]), Planet Labs PBC (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]), and more.

FRIDAY, MARCH 20

XPENG (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV])

XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]) is set to report FQ4 results before the market opens on Friday, with analysts expecting profits to improve by about 96% Y/Y on more than 43% revenue growth.

Recently, Morgan Stanley shared positive remarks [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562356-xpeng-rallies-after-showing-off-its-new-next-gen-autonomous-driving-model] after testing the company’s VLA 2.0 autonomous driving system in Guangzhou. The next-generation Vision-Language-Action AI model is designed as an end-to-end vision-to-action stack aimed at enabling Level 4-grade autonomous driving and broader embodied AI applications. The technology is expected to roll out on XPeng’s P7, G7, and X9 Ultra models in the second half of the year.

Earlier this month, XPeng reported [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4559074-xpeng-february-deliveries-slide-499-yy-to-15256-units-following-lunar-new-year-global-p7-rollout-begins] February deliveries of 15,256 vehicles, down 49.9% Y/Y and about 23% lower than January’s 20,011 units, highlighting ongoing volatility in monthly sales trends.

Just days ahead of earnings, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating upgraded the stock to Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating] from Hold, aligning with bullish sell-side sentiment.

Seeking Alpha contributor Michael Ngan also maintains a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4862203-xpeng-not-just-a-car-company], noting that XPeng’s performance since the second half of 2024 supports its competitive positioning. While domestic EV sales growth may stabilize, the analyst sees additional upside from emerging businesses such as AeroHT, robotaxis, and humanoid robotics, which could add about $7B in enterprise value. Ngan sets a price target of $25.50 for XPEV.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: -$0.01
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $3.19B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 7 of the past 8 quarters and revenue estimates in 4 of those reports.

MORE ON RELATED STOCKS:

 	* Micron Q2 Earnings Preview: Expect Another Monster Quarter [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4882244-micron-q2-earnings-preview-expect-another-monster-quarter]
 	* Macy's Q4 Preview: High Probability Of An Earnings Beat, Buying The Stock [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4882223-macys-q4-preview-high-probability-earnings-beat-buying-stock]
 	* Micron: Dirt Cheap Before Earnings [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4882216-micron-dirt-cheap-before-earnings]
 	* Micron in focus as Wedbush ups price target on stronger-than-expected pricing [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4564254-micron-in-focus-as-wedbush-ups-price-target-on-stronger-than-expected-pricing]
 	* Alibaba debuts OpenClaw app, intensifying China’s agentic AI race after Baidu’s DuClaw launch [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4564148-alibaba-debuts-openclaw-app-intensifying-chinas-agentic-ai-race-after-baidus-duclaw-launch]

title: GSK wins FDA label expansion for RSV vaccine, Arexvy
content:
[GSK -Glaxosmithkline headquarters office building in Poznan.]
Magdalena Wygralak

GSK (GSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSK]) announced on Friday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has approved a label expansion for its Arexvy vaccine against the Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) to allow its use for those aged 18 to 49 years.

Accordingly, the single-dose shot will be available in the U.S. for those in that age group who are at an elevated risk for lower respiratory tract disease (LRTD) caused by the RSV.

Arexvy, which contains an adjuvant licensed from Antigenics Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of Agenus (AGEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AGEN]), became the first U.S.-cleared shot for RSV in May 2023, as the FDA approved it for people aged 60 years and older to prevent LRTD caused by the virus. [https://seekingalpha.com/news/3964734-gsks-arexvy-becomes-first-us-cleared-rsv-vaccine]

A year later, the approval was expanded to cover adults aged 50–59 who are at an increased risk for LRTD caused by RSV. [https://www.gsk.com/en-gb/media/press-releases/gsk-s-rsv-vaccine-arexvy-approved-in-us-for-expanded-age-indication-in-adults-aged-18-49-years-at-increased-risk/] Arexvy is not indicated for use during pregnancy. Pfizer (PFE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PFE]) and Moderna (MRNA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRNA]) also market FDA-approved RSV vaccines in the U.S.

MORE ON GSK, AGENUS

 	* GSK plc (GSK) Presents at Barclays 28th Annual Global Healthcare Conference Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4881291-gsk-plc-gsk-presents-at-barclays-28th-annual-global-healthcare-conference-transcript]
 	* GSK: Post-Pivot Buoyancy Set To Continue After Strong 2025 (Upgrade) [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4870939-gsk-post-pivot-buoyancy-set-to-continue-after-strong-2025-upgrade]
 	* GSK plc 2025 Q4 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4866251-gsk-plc-2025-q4-results-earnings-call-presentation]
 	* GSK gains approval of leucovorin for rare genetic disorder [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562754-gsk-gains-approval-leucovorin-rare-genetic-disorder]
 	* Agenus receives $20M payment under Zydus Life Sciences collaboration [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4562667-agenus-receives-20m-payment-under-zydus-life-sciences-collaboration]

title: LGND: New Year Program Updates
content:
By John Vandermosten, CFA

NASDAQ:LGND

READ THE FULL LGND RESEARCH REPORT

In the wake of the December analyst day, Ligand Pharmaceuticals, Inc.’s (NASDAQ:LGND) partners have seen meaningful activity with another acquisition for Pelthos, a delay in Filspari’s Target Action Date, a drug application acceptance for Ohtuvayre in China, positive results for Palvella’s portfolio, and a strategic collaboration for BOT/ BAL. Ligand also made an investment in Athira (now LeonaBio) as part of a consortium in support of the acquisition of lasofoxifene. In this report, we will update investors on each of these and provide our updated thoughts on the company.

Pelthos

Ligand owns about half of the equity in Pelthos Therapeutics, and it is entitled to a royalty from Zelsuvmi and Xepi, the latter of which was acquired in November 2025. A January 2026 press release announced that Pelthos had acquired Xeglyze, which is a topical treatment for head lice. The drug was approved by the FDA in July 2020, and Pelthos plans to relaunch Xeglyze in 1H:27. Oriented towards children, the product is complementary to Pelthos’ other two products that treat molluscum contagiosum and impetigo. While Ligand does not earn a royalty on Xeglyze sales, it owns a substantial equity interest that will benefit from the company’s overall performance.

Filspari

Ligand has received $25.8 million of royalty revenues over the trailing twelve months from the sale of Travere’s Filspari. The drug is approved for use to slow kidney function decline in adults with primary immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN) who are at risk for disease progression. Ligand’s partner, Travere, has sought to obtain approval for Filspari in a new indication called Focal Segmental Glomerulosclerosis (FSGS) over several years. Since 2023, Travere had been working on a submission of a supplemental NDA for Filspari for use in FSGS. In May 2025, the FDA accepted the application and assigned a Target Action Date of January 13th, 2026.

In the weeks prior to the anticipated approval, the FDA made a series of information requests to clarify the benefit of Filspari in FSGS. While the responses were submitted prior to the agency’s decision, there was not sufficient time for the agency to properly review the data. As a result, the FDA delayed the Target Action Date by three months, classifying the additional data submission as a Major Amendment. Travere management clarified that the data requested did not include anything related to safety or manufacturing of the drug.

It stands to reason that the agency would need additional time to review the responses to its questions; however, the risk of a complete response letter remains. Some analysts have cited historical data showing a better than even chance related to delays such as this that the candidate in question will be approved.

Story Continues

We have not explicitly forecasted FSGS revenues in our model, but Ligand does include a component for FSGS revenues in their Pharm Team segment. In its Analyst Day presentation last December, Ligand notes that contribution from Filspari for FSGS is an estimated $4 million in 2026, rising to $40-$45 million in 2030.

Lasofoxifene

Lasofoxifene was first discovered in 1992 through a research collaboration between Ligand and Pfizer. It is a type of endocrine and a selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM). In breast cancer, SERMs work by sitting in the estrogen receptors in breast cells, thus blocking the effects of estrogen in the breast tissue. They also function as estrogens in bone and protect against osteoporosis. Ligand is eligible for milestones and royalties from lasofoxifene that range from 6% to 10%.

On December 18th, 2025, Athira Pharma (now doing business as LeonaBio) announced that it had acquired an exclusive license to develop lasofoxifene for metastatic breast cancer in a Phase III program. The company executed a financing for $236 million, which included $90 million upfront and another $146 million if associated warrants are exercised. Ligand participated as a minor investor in the financing, along with leadership from Commodore Capital, Perceptive Advisors, and TCGX. The ongoing Phase III trial is over half enrolled and is expected to generate data in 2027.

QTORIN Rapamycin

Ligand holds a royalty interest in Palvella Therapeutics’ lead program, QTORIN rapamycin, a topical mTOR inhibitor for rare, serious dermatologic conditions like pachyonychia congenita and microcystic lymphatic malformations. The royalty was increased to a tiered range of 8.0 to 9.8% in 2023 as Ligand expanded its interest in Qtorin rapamycin to include other derivatives of the platform. Ligand also holds preferred stock in Palvella.

In January, Palvella provided an update on its portfolio, noting that its Phase III SELVA study is on track for a March 2026 report of topline data. Assuming that the data are positive, a New Drug Application (NDA) submission is expected in 2H:26 for the indication of microcystic lymphatic malformations. Phase II results for Qtorin were announced in December 2025 for clinically significant angiokeratomas, following which, management requested a Preliminary Breakthrough Therapy Designation Advice meeting with the FDA. The meeting is expected in 1Q:26.

Ohtuvayre

Nuance Pharm announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China has officially accepted for review the NDA for Ohtuvayre (ensifentrine) for the maintenance treatment of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. In 2021, Nuance Pharma entered into an agreement with Verona Pharma for the exclusive rights to develop and commercialize Ohtuvayre in Greater China (mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan). According to the NMPA website, the review timeline for drug marketing authorization applications is 200 days.

Botensilimab and Balstilimab (BOT+BAL)

Agenus closed on its planned strategic collaboration with Zydus Lifesciences to commercialize BOT/BAL in India and Sri Lanka. The deal included upfront monies, an equity investment in Agenus, future milestones, and royalties that will support the development of manufacturing capacity in the United States.

Summary

With a large portfolio of assets, Ligand investment companies are bound to have material news at a regular cadence. Since mid-December, we have seen a number of updates for Pelthos, Filspari, lasofoxifene, Qtorin rapamycin, and Ohtuvayre. With the exception of the delay in the Target Action Date for Filspari, the other updates were positive. Pelthos continues to expand its portfolio of offerings and now has a treatment for head lice, while a drug developed during Ligand’s earliest days is pursuing a new indication in metastatic breast cancer. Palvella is advancing Qtorin rapamycin in multiple indications, all of which will pay royalties to Ligand on revenues generated. Ohtuvayre has made great strides in China and has already been approved in several Pilot Zones and is now being put in front of the NMPA for approval in mainland China. Agenus has closed its planned collaboration with Zydus for the latter to commercialize in India and Sri Lanka.

title: Agenus and Zydus complete $141m BOT+BAL immunotherapy collaboration deal
content:
Agenus has completed a $141m strategic collaboration with Zydus Lifesciences to accelerate the global development and potential commercialisation of its botensilimab and balstilimab (BOT/BAL) immunotherapy combination programme.

The collaboration secures funding and sustained biologics manufacturing capacity in the US for Agenus, supporting clinical development, early access initiatives, and future commercial production.

Under the agreement, Agenus has provided Zydus with exclusive rights to develop and commercialise BOT/BAL in Sri Lanka and India, with Agenus eligible for a 5% royalty on net sales from these markets.

Key financial terms include a $75m upfront cash payment to Agenus for transferring manufacturing sites in Emeryville and Berkeley, California, a $16m equity investment by Zydus in Agenus common stock (around 2.1 million shares at $7.50 per share), and up to $50m in contingent milestone payments triggered by production orders of BOT+BAL.

Last year, the BOT+BAL combination showed a two-year overall survival rate of 42% and a mature median overall survival of 21 months in an expanded cohort of 123 patients with third-line or later microsatellite-stable metastatic colorectal cancer without active liver metastases.

Agenus has initiated the global BATTMAN Phase III trial with the Canadian Cancer Trials Group, with sites activated for patient enrolment.

Following the closing, manufacturing facilities in Emeryville and Berkeley will transfer to Zydus under the newly formed Zylidac Bio subsidiary.

Agenus retains committed manufacturing capacity at these US sites for global access programmes, commercialisation, and clinical trials.

Agenus received advisory support from Porrima and Biotech Value Advisors (BVA) on partner selection, transaction structure, and negotiations.

Agenus chairman and CEO Dr Garo Armen said: “Closing this collaboration with Zydus strengthens our balance sheet and, critically, secures dedicated US manufacturing capacity at a pivotal moment for Agenus.

“With these foundations in place, our focus in 2026 is disciplined execution, advancing our Phase III programme, broadening paid patient access through authorised pathways, and progressing toward regulatory submission supported by one of the most substantial clinical datasets generated in MSS colorectal cancer.”

In June 2025, Agenus entered a $141m partnership with Zydus Lifesciences and its subsidiaries to strengthen global manufacturing, advance clinical development, and expand access to BOT/BAL.

"Agenus and Zydus complete $141m BOT+BAL immunotherapy collaboration deal" was originally created and published by Hospital Management, a GlobalData owned brand.
